document.title: Geography Extension Catalog — Heritage Gold → LAWIM_V2
document.description: Enriched catalog of all geography model extensions required for LAWIM_V2, covering African real estate geography with 14 hierarchical levels, alias resolution, zone-based CRM routing, geographic scoring, mobility modes, and market equivalents

geographic_levels.description: 14-level geographic hierarchy covering administrative boundaries and operational groupings for African real estate

values.description: Sovereign state — top of the geographic hierarchy
First-level administrative division (state, province, region)
Second-level administrative division
Legal municipal entity with elected government
Sub-municipal administrative district
Urban settlement that may span multiple municipalities
Named residential or commercial area within a city
LAWIM-defined grouping of neighborhoods for agent routing and market analysis
Linear geographic corridor along a major road or boulevard
Notable point of interest used as geographic reference
values.example: Marché Mfoundi, Hôpital Central, Université Yaoundé I

values.description: Rural settlement outside municipal boundaries
Group of geographically related units for matching optimization
values.example: Cluster Yaoundé Nord, Cluster Douala Centre

values.description: Precise GPS coordinate reference point, may belong to any level
Non-hierarchical alias entry for name resolution, maps variant names to canonical units

alias_types.description: 10 alias types for the geographic alias resolution system, ordered by confidence impact

values.description: Primary official name of the geographic unit
Recognized alternative official name (administrative variant, bilingual name)
Common local spelling or pronunciation variant
Compound name with extra geographic qualifier
Social media, SMS, or chat abbreviation
Historical name no longer in official use
Legacy system name from Heritage Gold or previous data sources
Known typographical error from user input history
Name translated into another language
Phonetic approximation based on pronunciation

geographic_scoring.description: 5-level geographic scoring system for match precision between demandeur location and property location

levels.label: High
levels.description: Same zone or compatible axis — nearby neighborhoods

levels.label: Medium
levels.description: Same cluster within the same city

levels.label: Low
levels.description: Same city, different cluster — accessible but not close

levels.label: Minimal
levels.description: City affinity match — different but compatible cities

mobility_modes.description: 3 mobility modes defining travel thresholds for proximity matching and commute compatibility

values.label: Low (Walk)
values.description: Walking distance — properties within walkable range for daily commute

values.label: Medium (Drive)
values.description: Short driving distance — standard urban commute range
default_selection_rules: Default mode when no preference specified

values.label: High (Regional)
values.description: Regional commuting — acceptable for investment or cross-city opportunities

relation_types.description: 8 geographic relation types for typed connections between geographic units

values.description: Geographic units in close proximity (≤ 2 km walking distance)
Parent-child containment relationship (derived from parent_id hierarchy)
values.inference_method: derived from parent_id field on GeographicUnit

values.description: Units sharing a border or immediately adjacent to each other
values.inference_method: inferred from shared edges in geometry (GeoJSON boundary intersection)

values.description: Units located along the same major road, boulevard, or geographic corridor
Units grouped into the same LAWIM-defined cluster for optimized matching
values.inference_method: manually curated based on affinity and proximity

values.description: Units within acceptable commute distance based on mobility mode thresholds
values.inference_method: computed from mobility mode thresholds (5/20/50+ km)

values.description: Official administrative hierarchy relationship (government-defined boundaries)
Cross-market equivalent units with similar real estate characteristics
values.inference_method: manually curated with multi-dimensional similarity scoring

market_equivalent_dimensions.description: 6 similarity dimensions used to compute market equivalence scores between geographic units

dimensions.description: Average price per square meter for relevant property types
Urban vs suburban vs rural character
Income levels, age distribution, household size

lifecycle.description: Market equivalent lifecycle stages from suggestion to deprecation

stages.description: AI-suggested equivalence based on algorithmic similarity detection

stages.trigger: Admin or domain expert confirms equivalence

stages.description: Active for cross-market matching and price estimation
No longer considered equivalent due to market changes

relation_inference_rules.description: Automated inference rules for geographic relations

rules.description: If unit A has parent_id = unit B, then B CONTAINS A
If geometry polygons share an edge or vertex, units are adjacent
If GPS distance ≤ 2 km (haversine), units are nearby
If distance ≤ mobility mode max distance, units are commute compatible
Axes are defined per city by domain experts
Clusters group neighborhoods with high affinity
Cross-market equivalence computed from 6 similarity dimensions, requires validation

geo_confidence_calculation.description: Composite confidence calculation for geographic resolution quality

zone_model.description: Zone-based CRM routing model for agent assignment

geographic_unit_ids.description: Geographic units covered by this zone

is_active.description: Whether zone is active for routing

routing_priority.description: Priority when multiple zones match (lower = higher)

routing_methods.description: Match lead city/neighborhood to zone geographic_unit_ids. Route to agent with lowest current lead count in matching zone.
Route to agent with lowest current_leads / max_leads ratio who is online and zone-eligible.
Route to agent with highest agent_rating in the matching zone or general pool.
Admin manually assigns lead to an agent from the organization member list.

extensions.current_limitation: V2 has no unified geographic unit model. Cities, neighborhoods, and zones are scattered across string fields with no hierarchy, aliasing, or canonical identification.
extensions.proposed_structure: Create GeographicUnit entity with canonical_id (String), name (String), aliases (String[]), level (Enum: 14 values), parent_id (String?), coordinates (GeoJSON Point?), geometry (GeoJSON?), source (Enum: official|recovered|inferred|user_provided|ai_generated), confidence (Float 0.0-1.0), last_verified_at (DateTime?), status (Enum: active|deprecated|draft|pending_verification). Each geographic location is a GeographicUnit record at its appropriate level.
required_relations: GeographicUnit → GeographicRelation: 1:N (relations where this unit participates)
Property → GeographicUnit: N:1 (property references its location)
required_permissions: GEO: read geographic units (all authenticated users)
required_states: GeographicUnit.level ∈ {country, region, department, municipality, district, city, neighborhood, zone, axis, landmark, village, cluster, gps_point, geographic_alias}
extensions.human_decision_question: Geographic level definitions: 14 levels as specified (GEOGRAPHY_EXTENSION_MODEL.md §2.1) or collapsed set for MVP?

extensions.source_concept: Geographic hierarchy with administrative and operational levels
extensions.current_limitation: V2 has only city/neighborhood string fields with no hierarchical structure. No support for administrative levels (region, department, municipality) or operational groupings (zone, axis, cluster).
extensions.proposed_structure: Implement 14-level hierarchy: administrative levels (country→region→department→municipality→district) + operational levels (city→neighborhood→zone→axis→landmark→village→cluster) + reference levels (gps_point→geographic_alias). Hierarchy enforced via parent_id self-reference with level validation. Every unit must have a complete parent chain to country. Operational units (zone, axis, cluster) may have context-dependent parent bindings.
required_states: Level-validated parent-child relationship: child.level must be > parent.level
Administrative chain must be complete to country level
extensions.human_decision_question: Hierarchy scope: full 14-level hierarchy for all African markets, or start with Cameroon-only collapsed hierarchy (8 levels omitting zone/axis/landmark/village/cluster)?

extensions.current_limitation: V2 has no alias resolution system. Location matching uses simple string comparison with limited typo correction. No learning mechanism for new location names.
extensions.proposed_structure: Create GeographicAlias entity: id (UUID), canonical_unit_id (String), alias (String), alias_type (Enum: 10 types), language (String?), source (String), confidence (Float 0.0-1.0), occurrences (Int), last_seen_at (DateTime?), created_at (DateTime), status (Enum: active|deprecated|pending_review). Create LocationSynonym table for learned mappings: input_text (String), canonical_id (String), match_type (Enum: exact|fuzzy|compound|manual), match_distance (Int), confidence (Float), occurrences (Int), last_matched_at (DateTime). Implement 5-stage resolution pipeline: normalization → exact match → fuzzy match (Levenshtein ≤ 3) → compound extraction → knowledge learning. Auto-create aliases at 5 occurrences with 0.50 confidence.
required_states: GeographicAlias.alias_type ∈ {canonical, official_alias, common_variant, social_variant, typo, historical, legacy, compound, translation, phonetic}
extensions.human_decision_question: Fuzzy matching threshold: Gold uses Levenshtein ≤ 3. Accept default or adjust per market?

extensions.current_limitation: V2 has no zone concept. Lead routing uses simple round-robin without geographic awareness.
extensions.proposed_domain: Domain 10: CRM Pipeline + Domain 12: Organization Model
extensions.proposed_structure: Create Zone entity: zone_id (String), name (String), organization_id (UUID), member_ids (UUID[]), geographic_unit_ids (String[]), is_active (Boolean), routing_priority (Int), max_leads_per_agent (Int), created_at (DateTime). Extend OrganizationMember with zones (String[]) referencing Zone.zone_id. Implement 4 routing methods: zone-based (primary), availability-based (fallback), score-based (tiebreaker), manual (admin). Decision tree: resolve unit → find matching zones → single match route / multiple match by priority / no match fallback to availability.
required_permissions: CRM: assign zones to agent (org admin/responsible)
CRM: view zone assignments (org admin, agent self)
extensions.human_decision_question: Zone routing strategy: multi-level zone assignment (city-wide, cluster, specific neighborhoods) or flat per-city zones? Gold specifies zone-based with availability fallback.

extensions.current_limitation: V2 has binary city/neighborhood match with fixed weights. No graduated scoring for partial geographic matches.
extensions.proposed_structure: Implement 5-level geographic scoring: Level 1 — Exact (1.00, same neighborhood), Level 2 — High (0.85, same zone/axis), Level 3 — Medium (0.60, same cluster), Level 4 — Low (0.30, same city different cluster), Level 5 — Minimal (0.10, city affinity). Formula: geo_score = base_weight × geo_level_weight where base_weight defaults to 30% of total match score. Geographic scoring components: city match (30% of geo weight), neighborhood match (40%), zone/cluster match (20%), GPS proximity (10%). Level determination via shareZone(), shareAxis(), shareCluster(), hasAffinity() functions.
required_permissions: MATCH: read geo scoring config (admin only)
extensions.human_decision_question: Geographic score weights: Gold baseline (exact 1.00, high 0.85, medium 0.60, low 0.30, minimal 0.10) as specified in GEOGRAPHY_EXTENSION_MODEL.md §5.1, or adjusted values per market segment?

extensions.current_limitation: V2 has no mobility context. Proximity scoring uses fixed distance without mode differentiation.
extensions.proposed_structure: Implement 3 mobility modes: Low/Walk (5 km, 5 km/h, walking), Medium/Drive (20 km, 30 km/h urban, default), High/Regional (50+ km, 80 km/h inter-urban). Mode selection: explicit user preference, inferred from property type (investment → high), urgency (urgent rental → low), diaspora profile (→ high). Scoring: within max_distance → 1.0; beyond → decay max(0, 1 - (excess/max)); hard cutoff at 2× max. Mobility score integrated into overall geographic score as a multiplier. COMMUTE_COMPATIBLE relation derived from mobility thresholds.
extensions.human_decision_question: Mobility distance thresholds: Gold defaults (low=5km, medium=20km, high=50km) as specified in GEOGRAPHY_EXTENSION_MODEL.md §6.1, or adjusted for African urban context?

extensions.current_limitation: V2 has no relation system between geographic units. Affinity matrices and proximity are computed ad-hoc without persistent relation storage.
extensions.proposed_structure: Create GeographicRelation entity: id (UUID), source_unit_id (String), target_unit_id (String), relation_type (Enum: nearby|contains|adjacent|same_axis|same_cluster|commute_compatible|administrative_parent|market_equivalent), direction (Enum: bidirectional|source_to_target|target_to_source), weight (Float 0.0-1.0), metadata (JSON? — includes distance_km, estimated_travel_time, road_quality), source (String: official|inferred|manual), valid_from (DateTime), valid_until (DateTime?), status (Enum: active|deprecated). Implement inference rules: CONTAINS from parent_id hierarchy, ADJACENT from geometry borders, NEARBY from GPS proximity (≤ 2 km), COMMUTE_COMPATIBLE from mobility thresholds. SAME_AXIS, SAME_CLUSTER, MARKET_EQUIVALENT are manually curated.
required_states: GeographicRelation.relation_type ∈ {nearby, contains, adjacent, same_axis, same_cluster, commute_compatible, administrative_parent, market_equivalent}

extensions.current_limitation: V2 has no cross-market equivalence concept. Properties from different cities or neighborhoods cannot be compared or matched based on similarity.
extensions.proposed_structure: Extend GeographicRelation with MARKET_EQUIVALENT type. Compute similarity from 6 dimensions: population_density (25%), economic_activity (20%), real_estate_price_tier (20%), infrastructure_level (15%), urbanization_pattern (10%), demographic_profile (10%). Lifecycle: suggested (AI-detected, not active) → validated (expert reviewed, not active) → active (available for matching) → deprecated (market change). Examples: Bastos↔Bonapriso (0.85), Mvog-Mbi↔Deido (0.75), Odza↔Makepe (0.70). Cross-market expansion workflow: no exact properties → find MARKET_EQUIVALENT relations → include with weight as similarity score → present with annotation.
required_states: GeographicRelation.source ∈ {suggested, validated, active, deprecated} (for MARKET_EQUIVALENT type)
extensions.human_decision_question: Market equivalent validation workflow: AI-suggested with human validation (as specified in GEOGRAPHY_EXTENSION_MODEL.md §8.4) or fully automated for high-confidence pairs?

extensions.source_concept: GPS point system with precision levels
extensions.current_limitation: V2 GPS data has no precision metadata, source tracking, or confidence levels. Only 62.6% of neighborhoods have GPS data with unknown quality.
extensions.proposed_structure: Extend GeographicUnit model with gps_point level for coordinate storage. Implement GPS precision levels: HIGH (< 50m), MEDIUM (50-500m), LOW (> 500m), UNKNOWN (no metadata). GPS resolution fallback chain: 1) neighborhood GPS exact, 2) neighborhood centroid inferred, 3) city GPS coordinates, 4) region GPS centroid, 5) null GPS with UNKNOWN accuracy. Store GPS metadata: source (String), precision_level (Enum), confidence (Float), last_verified_at (DateTime?). GPS coverage target: 100% of priority cities, 80% of secondary cities.
required_fields: GeographicUnit.gps_source: String? (data file or service name)

extensions.current_limitation: V2 has no confidence calculation for geographic resolution. Successful match is binary; no quality metric.
extensions.proposed_structure: Implement composite confidence calculation: resolution_confidence = base × alias_factor × gps_factor × ambiguity_factor. Base values: exact (1.0), alias (0.85), fuzzy (0.70), compound (0.75), unresolved (0.0). Alias factors: verified (1.0), unverified (0.90), typo (0.80). GPS factors: HIGH (1.0), MEDIUM (0.85), LOW (0.60), UNKNOWN (0.30). Ambiguity factors: unique (1.0), resolved (0.50), unresolved (0.0). Store computed confidence on GeographicUnit.confidence. Recalculate on alias addition, GPS update, or verification event.

extensions.source_concept: Location synonym table for learned resolution
extensions.current_limitation: V2 has limited location synonym coverage. No automated learning mechanism for new location names from user input.
extensions.proposed_structure: Extend LocationSynonym table with standardized schema: input_text (String — raw user input), canonical_id (String — resolved unit), match_type (Enum: exact|fuzzy|compound|manual), match_distance (Int — Levenshtein distance, 0 for exact), confidence (Float 0.0-1.0), occurrences (Int — usage count), last_matched_at (DateTime), created_at (DateTime). Implement learning pipeline: on unresolved location → store as candidate → increment occurrences → at 5 occurrences → auto-create alias with 0.50 confidence in GeographicAlias → notify admin for review.
required_permissions: GEO: read location synonyms (all authenticated users)
extensions.human_decision_question: Auto-learning policy: auto-create alias at 5 occurrences with 0.50 confidence (as specified in GEOGRAPHY_EXTENSION_MODEL.md §3.2) or require admin review at any confidence level?

extensions.current_limitation: V2 has no automated relation inference. All geographic affinities and proximities must be manually curated.
extensions.proposed_structure: Implement automated relation inference engine. CONTAINS: derived directly from GeographicUnit.parent_id. ADJACENT: inferred from geometry edge intersection (GeoJSON boundary analysis). NEARBY: computed from GPS haversine distance ≤ 2 km. COMMUTE_COMPATIBLE: computed from GPS distance ≤ mobility mode threshold. ADJACENT, NEARBY, and COMMUTE_COMPATIBLE relations are batch-computed on a schedule (daily/weekly). Batch inference creates relations with source='inferred' and status='active'. Manual relations (SAME_AXIS, SAME_CLUSTER, MARKET_EQUIVALENT) are excluded from automation. Inference logs track created/deprecated relations.
required_fields: GeographicRelation.inference_rule: String? (which rule produced this relation)
required_permissions: SYSTEM: run relation inference (geo engine service)

related_extensions.zone_based_routing: EXT-CRM-005 (CRM routing engine — zone method uses Zone entity)
related_extensions.proximity_scoring: EXT-PROP-008 (data quality scoring includes location completeness)
